rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when generating AGREE contracts
Do not insert AGREE annex blocks that reference subcomponent ports in component type scopes
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL structure during model fusion
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in the correct scope for their intended purpose
Use only identifiers that are visible in the current scope
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports in component type annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
Use valid component type scopes for component port references
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annex blocks
Do not use identifiers that are not visible in the current scope
Do not insert AGREE annex blocks that are not properly scoped
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
